words.definition: To move closer to someone or something.
words.example: The train is approaching the station.

words.definition: If a ball or other object bounces, it hits a surface and immediately moves away from it.
words.example: The ball bounced off the wall and hit the window.

words.definition: To move along the ground on your hands and knees, or with your body close to the ground.
words.example: The baby started to crawl at six months old.

words.definition: To rest or move slowly on the surface of a liquid and not sink.
words.example: Leaves float on the surface of the lake.

words.definition: If a liquid flows, it moves smoothly and continuously in one direction.
words.example: The river flows from the mountains to the sea.

words.definition: To move forward by jumping on one foot.
words.example: The child hopped along the pavement on one leg.

words.definition: To run at a slow steady speed, usually for exercise or pleasure.
words.example: She jogs in the park every morning.

words.definition: To move forward while turning over and over.
words.example: The ball rolled down the hill and into the garden.

words.definition: To disappear below the surface of the water.
words.example: The heavy stone sank to the bottom of the lake.

words.definition: To move forwards by jumping first on one foot and then the other.
words.example: The children were skipping in the playground.

words.definition: To move smoothly and quickly across a surface.
words.example: The children love to slide down the snowy hill.

words.definition: If you slip, your feet slide accidentally and you lose your balance or fall over.
words.example: Be careful not to slip on the wet floor.

words.definition: To move by putting one foot down in front of the other.
words.example: Step carefully over the rocks to avoid falling.

words.definition: To hit your foot on something and fall down.
words.example: She tripped on the carpet and fell.

words.definition: To move your hand to say hello or goodbye, or as a signal.
words.example: He waved goodbye as the train left the station.
